weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like the 'ee' in 'see'. Single short vowel.

monday.formal_usage: Formal: 'iie' is itself the formal/polite no.
monday.sentence_seed: When someone offers you something you don't want, say いいえ.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'ie' refers to your own home casually.
tuesday.formal_usage: Formal: 'otaku' (お宅) is more polite for someone else's home.
tuesday.sentence_seed: Saying you're going home, you might use いえ.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'iku' as a plain verb, e.g. 'iku yo' (I'm going).
wednesday.sentence_seed: One of the most-used verbs in Japanese.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'ima' drops easily into casual sentences.
thursday.formal_usage: Formal: same word, same usage in polite speech.
thursday.sentence_seed: 'Ima' is what you say when something is happening right this moment.

friday_funword.description: The classic Japanese way of saying 'nice!' or 'sounds good!'. Drops into texts and chats constantly. The same phrase Facebook used for its 'like' button in Japan.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When a friend tells you their plan, the natural reply is いいね.
friday_funword.fun_note: Captain, this is the single most useful piece of Japanese small-talk vocabulary you'll learn. Use it freely.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like 'no' in 'note', short and clean.

monday.casual_usage: Casual: 'mono' for physical things or abstract things.
monday.sentence_seed: One of the most general nouns in Japanese.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'nomu' plain form, common in conversations.
tuesday.sentence_seed: Used for water, tea, alcohol, even medicine.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'noru' for trains, buses, bikes.
wednesday.sentence_seed: Used for any vehicle you board, including elevators.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: less common in casual speech, more written/formal.
thursday.formal_usage: Formal: appears in weather forecasts ('hare nochi ame' - sunny then rain).
thursday.sentence_seed: Common in scheduling and forecasting.

friday_funword.description: Pure vibes word. Describes someone who's relaxed and unhurried, or how a slow day feels. Often paired with する (suru) - 'nonbiri suru' means to chill out.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When the weekend has no plans and you're loving it, that's のんびり.
friday_funword.fun_note: The Japanese ideal of an unhurried, calm life is wrapped up in this one word.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like 'ta' in 'taco', short and crisp.

monday.sentence_seed: Top 5 most-used verb in daily Japanese.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'atatakai' or shortened 'attakai' for warm weather, food, people.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: works as adjective for both meanings, context decides.
wednesday.sentence_seed: Pulls double duty: a building is takai, and so is the bill.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'tanoshii' or 'tanoshikatta' (was fun).
thursday.sentence_seed: How you describe a good time afterwards.

friday_funword.description: Simple, useful, and the word also nicknames things that are smooth, oval, or in their early stages — a young trainee can be called a tamago.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: From breakfast to slang for a beginner, たまご is everywhere.
friday_funword.fun_note: In Japanese culture, calling someone a tamago is calling them a fresh recruit — not insulting, just new.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like 'o' in 'go', short. Often used as a polite prefix on nouns.

monday.sentence_seed: First thing you say to anyone in the morning.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'ocha' covers green tea by default.
tuesday.formal_usage: Formal: 'ocha' is itself polite (the お is the polite prefix).
tuesday.sentence_seed: Default beverage offered to guests.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'okane' is the everyday word.
wednesday.formal_usage: Formal: same word, the お makes it polite already.
wednesday.sentence_seed: The お prefix is built in - 'kane' alone sounds rough.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'osoi' as an adjective.
thursday.sentence_seed: Works for both 'you're late' and 'this is slow'.

friday_funword.translation: good work / you must be tired
friday_funword.description: Greeting between coworkers at end of the day, between teammates after a workout, between anyone who's just done something effortful. Untranslatable directly. Full version is otsukaresama deshita.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When a colleague finishes a long day, you say おつかれ.
friday_funword.fun_note: One of those words that fills a hole English doesn't have. Acknowledges effort without praising or pitying.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like 'a' in 'father', short and open.

monday.casual_usage: Casual: 'asa' as a noun referring to morning hours.
monday.sentence_seed: Used in time-of-day descriptions.

tuesday.sentence_seed: Same characters can also mean 'candy' with different pitch accent.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'ashita' very commonly used.
wednesday.formal_usage: Formal: 'asu' is more formal, often in writing.
wednesday.sentence_seed: Top time-reference word in conversation.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'atsui' for weather, food, also passion.
thursday.sentence_seed: Same word for hot weather, hot food, or hot topic.

friday_funword.description: The reaction sound. Used when you suddenly remember something, drop something, see something surprising, or want to interject. Tiny but constantly used in real Japanese conversation.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When you realize you forgot your keys, the reflex is あっ.
friday_funword.fun_note: Watch any Japanese show and count how many times this comes up. It's everywhere.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like 'kee' in 'key', short.

monday.sentence_seed: Most-used time word in daily speech.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'kiku' for both listening to music and asking a question.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'kirei' for beauty AND cleanliness.
wednesday.sentence_seed: Compliments people, places, and tidy rooms.

thursday.translation: want to ask / want to listen
thursday.casual_usage: Casual: '-tai' form expresses desire to do something.
thursday.sentence_seed: Useful when you have a question coming.

friday_funword.description: An onomatopoeia describing things that glitter, sparkle, or shimmer. Used for stars, jewels, eyes, anything shiny. Japanese has dozens of doubled-sound effect words and this is one of the most iconic.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: Stars at night are きらきら, and so are someone's excited eyes.
friday_funword.fun_note: There's a whole category of Japanese baby names called 'kirakira names' - flashy/unusual ones. The word itself feels playful.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like 'shee' in 'sheep', short. Note: し is 'shi' not 'si'.

monday.casual_usage: Casual: 'shiru' plain form, often used as 'shitteru' (knowing now).

similar_words: つとめる (tsutomeru) - to be employed

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'shizuka' as an adjective for quiet places.
wednesday.sentence_seed: Describes places, people, and atmospheres.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'shiawase' as a noun or adjective for deep happiness.
thursday.sentence_seed: Heavier, more meaningful happiness than 'tanoshii'.

friday_funword.description: Onomatopoeia for total silence. The classic 'pin-drop' moment in manga and anime - when nobody knows what to say, there's a shiin. Wonderfully, it's the sound of no sound.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: After a bad joke, the room goes しーん.
friday_funword.fun_note: A sound effect for the absence of sound. Japanese onomatopoeia is incredible.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like 'na' in 'nacho', short.

monday.casual_usage: Casual: 'nani' or even shorter 'nan' before certain sounds.
monday.formal_usage: Formal: 'nan desu ka' is the polite full form.
monday.sentence_seed: Most common question word in Japanese.

tuesday.sentence_seed: One of four season words you'll use constantly.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'namae' for general use; 'onamae' adds politeness.
wednesday.formal_usage: Formal: 'onamae' when asking someone their name.
wednesday.sentence_seed: First word in any introduction.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'naru' is one of the most useful verbs in Japanese.
thursday.sentence_seed: Used for becoming hot, cold, big, an adult - all of it.

friday_funword.description: The single best 'I get it' phrase in Japanese. Used when something just clicked. Drops into conversation naturally and shows engagement. Slightly understated, very natural.
similar_words: わかった (wakatta) - I got it
そうか (sou ka) - is that so
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When someone explains something well, you say なるほど.
friday_funword.fun_note: Sprinkle this into conversations and you'll instantly sound more natural.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'tsoo' - the t and s are blended together. No real English equivalent.

monday.sentence_seed: Used for making food, art, plans, anything.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'tsukau' for tools, money, time, language.
tuesday.sentence_seed: One of the most flexible verbs.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'tsuki' for both moon and the unit of a month.
wednesday.sentence_seed: Same word for celestial moon and calendar month.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'tsuyoi' for physical strength, willpower, flavors.
thursday.sentence_seed: Works for muscles, character, and even strong coffee.

friday_funword.description: Two meanings, both fun. Describes a smooth surface (a polished floor) AND the sound/feel of slurping noodles. Slurping noodles is encouraged in Japan, and the sound is つるつる.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: Ramen is meant to go つるつる as you eat it.
friday_funword.fun_note: A word that's both a texture and a sound. Slurp loudly, it's a compliment.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced like 'ma' in 'mama', short.

monday.sentence_seed: Hugely useful: 'chotto matte' means 'wait a sec'.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'mae' for spatial 'in front' or temporal 'before'.
tuesday.sentence_seed: Doubles for space and time.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'mainichi' as a time expression.
wednesday.sentence_seed: Mai- prefix means 'every'. Mainichi, maishuu, maitsuki.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'majime' as adjective for serious people, attitudes.
thursday.sentence_seed: A core compliment in Japanese culture - majime is admirable.

friday_funword.description: Doubled word with playful meanings: a baby is called marumaru if they're round and chubby (cute). Also means 'totally' or 'utterly'. Sometimes used as a placeholder like 'so-and-so' (Mr. Marumaru).
friday_funword.sentence_seed: A round, happy baby is まるまる, and so is a complete recovery.
friday_funword.fun_note: The character まる means circle and is a major cultural symbol of completeness in Japan.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'ree' but with the Japanese r - softer than English r, between r and l.

monday.sentence_seed: Aomori prefecture is famous for its ringo.

tuesday.sentence_seed: Used for the activity of cooking AND for cuisine type (Italian ryouri).

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'tsumori' as a noun for what you plan to do.
wednesday.formal_usage: Formal: 'tsumori desu' polite form. Used as 'verb + tsumori' = 'plan to verb'.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'giri' shows up in giri-choco (obligation chocolate on Valentine's).
thursday.formal_usage: Formal: 'giri' is itself a cultural concept; no different polite form.
thursday.sentence_seed: A core Japanese cultural concept of social obligation.

friday_funword.description: Borrowed from English 'relax', written in hiragana here for the lesson but normally written in katakana (リラックス). Shows how English loan words enter Japanese - often shortened, always pronounced phonetically.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: After a long week, time to りらっくす.
friday_funword.fun_note: Captain - this is a 'gairaigo' (loan word). Japanese has thousands. They're often easier to learn because you already know the English meaning.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'deh', short. The dakuten (small marks) makes て (te) into で (de).

monday.sentence_seed: Used for leaving home, exiting a building, even tears coming out.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'denwa' as the noun for phone.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'dekiru' or 'dekita' (was able to / done).
wednesday.sentence_seed: Top 10 verb. Used constantly.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: drops to 'na no?' or just rising tone in casual speech.
thursday.formal_usage: Formal: 'desu ka' is THE polite question ending.
thursday.sentence_seed: Attached to the end of polite questions.

friday_funword.description: Describes someone melting with affection - usually a tough character softening for someone they love. From the same family as 'tsundere' (cold-then-sweet) and 'yandere'. Anime culture word, but used in real life too.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When someone goes goofy over their crush, that's でれでれ.
friday_funword.fun_note: The -dere family of words from anime fandom has actually entered everyday Japanese.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'seh', short.

monday.formal_usage: Formal: 'sensei' is used to address teachers, doctors, mentors.
monday.sentence_seed: Used for teachers, doctors, and any expert mentor.

tuesday.sentence_seed: Used for 'around the world' phrases.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'semai' for tight or small spaces.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'seikatsu' for one's life routine.
thursday.formal_usage: Formal: same word, used in conversation and writing.
thursday.sentence_seed: Used in 'student life', 'married life', etc.

friday_funword.translation: going to all the trouble of
friday_funword.description: An untranslatable favorite. Means something like 'since you went to the trouble' or 'after all that effort'. Used when you want to honor effort that's been put in. Hard to teach, easy to use.
similar_words: わざわざ (wazawaza) - going out of one's way
がんばる (ganbaru) - to try hard
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When a friend cooked for you, eating it all is the せっかく thing to do.
friday_funword.fun_note: Captain - this word is one of those 'untranslatable Japanese' favorites. It packs a whole concept into three syllables.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'chee' like 'cheese', short.

monday.casual_usage: Casual: 'chiisai' as adjective, often shortened to 'chiisa'.

tuesday.sentence_seed: Spatial and temporal closeness both.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'chotto' is everywhere - 'a bit' or 'hold on'.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'chikara' for physical strength, ability, force.

friday_funword.description: Used to describe things appearing sparsely. Cherry blossoms starting to bloom - 'sakura ga chirahora saiteiru'. People scattered in a quiet park. Beautiful word for sparse, gentle occurrence.

friday_funword.sentence_seed: When the first leaves start changing color, they appear ちらほら.
friday_funword.fun_note: One of those Japanese words that paints a picture in two syllables.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'roh' with the Japanese r - softer than English r.

monday.sentence_seed: Used in 'what color' (nani iro) and color names.

tuesday.sentence_seed: Counter '-ji' attaches to numbers for o'clock.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'ofuro' standard word for bath/bathtub.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'kuro' as a noun (the color black) or component of 'kuroi' (adjective).

friday_funword.description: Describes something - or someone - in tatters. Old worn-out shoes are boroboro. After a brutal week, you can feel boroboro. Also used emotionally for crying so hard tears are everywhere.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: After moving day, the cardboard boxes are ぼろぼろ.
friday_funword.fun_note: Doubled words in Japanese (boroboro, kirakira, marumaru) are called gitaigo and they're a whole vocabulary world of their own.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'n' - the only standalone consonant in hiragana. Always at the end of a syllable, never the start.

monday.sentence_seed: Counter is also '-hon' for long thin objects.

tuesday.sentence_seed: Loan word that's been in Japanese for centuries.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'mikan' standard word.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'genki?' is a casual 'how are you?'.

similar_words: げんきいっぱい (genki ippai) - full of energy
thursday.sentence_seed: How you ask if someone's doing okay.

friday_funword.description: Onomatopoeia for steady rapid progress, knocking sound, drumming. 'Dondon yokunaru' means getting better and better. Versatile and very natural in conversation.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When you're improving fast, you're getting better どんどん.
friday_funword.fun_note: ん is a workhorse character. It shows up in roughly 1 in 5 Japanese words.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'neh', short. As a particle at end of sentences, means 'right?' or 'isn't it?'

monday.sentence_seed: Japan loves cats - the word is everywhere.

tuesday.sentence_seed: Same word for sleeping and lying down.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'nedan' for the price of something.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'negai' as a noun. 'Onegai' is the famous polite request word.

friday_funword.description: Doubled cute version of 'nemui' (sleepy). Used playfully to describe being drowsy. Not formal at all - very cute, very affectionate. Often used for kids or pets, or just being cute about being tired.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When the cat is curling up to nap, she's ねむねむ.
friday_funword.fun_note: Doubling words in Japanese makes them softer, cuter, more childlike. A whole register of Japanese.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'toh', short. Also functions as 'and' or 'with' as a particle.

monday.casual_usage: You learned this in Week 3. Revisiting for the と.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'tori' for birds in general AND chicken meat.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'tokoro' general word for place.

friday_funword.description: Two meanings. Knocking sound 'tonton' for tapping or knocking. AND 'tonton' to mean even or break-even. 'Shuushi tonton' means breaking even financially.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced between 'fu' and 'hu' - softer than English 'f', made with both lips.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'fuben' as adjective.

friday_funword.description: Pure delight word. Describes anything fluffy - clouds, pancakes, puppies, blankets. One of the most beloved gitaigo. Marshmallow Japanese.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'ha' as a syllable - but as the topic-marker particle, pronounced 'wa'. Important distinction.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'hayai' for both speed and earliness.

friday_funword.translation: anxiously / on the edge of your seat

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'roo' with Japanese r - softer than English r.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'suru' or 'shita' (did).

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'wakaru' or 'wakatta' (got it).

friday_funword.description: Spinning, circling, going in circles. Wrap a bandage guruguru. Get dizzy guruguru. Drive in circles guruguru. Endlessly useful and very fun to say.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'keh', short.

monday.sentence_seed: Used for doors, windows, eyes, packages, even the new year ('akemashite omedetou').

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'kesa' as a time expression for today's morning.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'ike' as a noun.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'kekkou' multi-purpose word.

friday_funword.description: Specifically describes loud, slightly silly laughing - cackling, bright laughter. 'Kerakera warau' is to laugh hard. Different from a polite chuckle.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: Watching slapstick comedy makes you laugh けらけら.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'soh', short.

tuesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'sore' for things near the listener.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'sou' or 'sou da yo' to confirm.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'soto' for outside in casual speech.
thursday.formal_usage: Formal: 'sotogawa' is more specific - the outer side of something.

friday_funword.description: Untranslatably useful. Used to softly signal 'we should probably get going' or 'it's time'. The polite Japanese way to start wrapping things up. 'Sorosoro kaerimashou' = 'we should head home soon'.

ぼちぼち (bochibochi) - little by little

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'moo', short.

monday.casual_usage: Casual: 'muzukashii' as adjective. Often shortened to 'muzui'.

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'mune' for the physical chest AND emotional heart.

friday_funword.description: Originally meant 'a complete mess' but young people use it like 'really' or 'super'. 'Muchakucha oishii' = 'super delicious'. It's chaotic energy as an intensifier.
similar_words: めちゃくちゃ (mechakucha) - same word, slangier
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When you really love something, you say むちゃくちゃ + adjective.
friday_funword.fun_note: Japanese slang for 'very/really' has at least ten variants. This is one of the more energetic.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'bah', short. The dakuten makes は (ha) into ば (ba).

monday.casual_usage: Casual: 'basho' for specific place or location.
monday.sentence_seed: More formal than 'tokoro' - means specific identifiable location.

tuesday.sentence_seed: Compound: 'ban' (evening) + 'gohan' (rice/meal).

wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'ganbaru' or 'ganbatte!' (do your best!).
wednesday.sentence_seed: Cultural cornerstone word. 'Ganbatte!' is the universal Japanese cheer.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'bara' for the flower rose.
thursday.sentence_seed: Symbol of love in Japanese same as in West, less culturally weighted than sakura.

friday_funword.description: Two-track word. Describes flapping (wings, fabric in wind) AND describes a hectic, rushed period of time. 'Saikin batabata shiteru' = 'I've been swamped lately'.
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When work piles up and you're running everywhere, you're ばたばた.
friday_funword.fun_note: Japanese gitaigo for emotional/social states are extraordinarily evocative.

weeks.character_sound_note: Pronounced 'yoh', short. As particle at end of sentences, adds emphasis or new information.

monday.sentence_seed: Late evening to night. 'Konban' is THIS evening specifically.

tuesday.sentence_seed: One of the foundational verbs.

wednesday.translation: please treat me well / nice to meet you
wednesday.casual_usage: Casual: 'yoroshiku' shortens many polite phrases.
wednesday.formal_usage: Formal: 'yoroshiku onegaishimasu' is the full polite phrase.
similar_words: はじめまして (hajimemashite) - nice to meet you
wednesday.sentence_seed: Among the most culturally important phrases. Said in introductions and at end of requests.

thursday.casual_usage: Casual: 'yokatta' for relief and gladness. Past tense of 'ii' (good).
thursday.sentence_seed: Captain - one of those phrases you'll find yourself reaching for constantly.

friday_funword.description: Describes a baby learning to walk - those tiny wobbling steps. Universally adorable. Also used affectionately for anyone learning something new and stumbling at first.
similar_words: よちよちあるき (yochiyochi aruki) - toddling walk
friday_funword.sentence_seed: When a baby is taking their first steps, they're walking よちよち.
friday_funword.fun_note: A perfect word to close out the foundation. You're learning Japanese yochiyochi - and that's exactly right.
